ICD-10-CM FY25 Guidelines October 1, 2024 (Older): Whether or not the patient delivers during the current hospitalization does not affect assignment of the POA indicator. -> icd (New Version): Whether or not the patient delivers during the current hospitalization does not affect assignment of the POA indicator.
ICD-10-CM FY25 Guidelines October 1, 2024 (Older): If the pregnancy complication or obstetrical condition was present on admission (e.g., patient admitted in preterm labor), assign 'Y'. -> icd (New Version): If the pregnancy complication or obstetrical condition was present on admission (e.g., patient admitted in preterm labor), assign 'Y'.

findings.summary: The older version (Chunk 242) and the new version (Chunk 670) present the exact same definition: a postpartum complication is any complication occurring within the six-week period following delivery. There are no differences in the clinical logic, time window, or coding requirements between the two text excerpts provided. The change type is Administrative Update because the content is identical, suggesting the update is likely a re-publication or formatting adjustment rather than a substantive guideline revision.
findings.detail: ICD-10-CM FY25 Guidelines October 1, 2024 (Older): A postpartum complication is any complication occurring within the sixweek period. -> icd (New Version): A postpartum complication is any complication occurring within the sixweek period.

findings.summary: The older version (Chunk 241) and the new version (Chunk 669) contain verbatim identical text regarding the six-week postpartum definition and the five-month peripartum scope. There are no behavioral changes, logic shifts, or clinical rule modifications between these two specific excerpts. The similarity suggests these are likely the same guideline text indexed under different internal identifiers or versions without substantive alteration.
findings.detail:
ICD-10-CM FY25 Guidelines October 1, 2024 (Older): The postpartum period begins immediately after delivery and continues for six weeks following delivery. -> icd (New Version): The postpartum period begins immediately after delivery and continues for six weeks following delivery.
ICD-10-CM FY25 Guidelines October 1, 2024 (Older): The peripartum period is defined as the last month of pregnancy to five months postpartum. -> icd (New Version): The peripartum period is defined as the last month of pregnancy to five months postpartum.

findings.summary: The core sequencing instruction in both versions is identical, mandating O9A.2 as the primary code followed by the specific injury or toxic effect. The only differences are minor administrative additions in the 'New items' section of the newer version, such as the explicit requirement to link the toxic effect to its origin via an external cause code and the clarification to code adverse effects as distinct entities. These additions do not alter the clinical logic or the primary sequencing hierarchy defined in the raw text excerpts.
findings.detail:
ICD-10-CM FY25 Guidelines October 1, 2024 (Older): Mandatory inclusion of the O9A.2 subcategory code as the primary diagnosis for complicating toxic effects -> icd (New Version): Mandatory inclusion of the specific external cause code to link the toxic effect to its origin
ICD-10-CM FY25 Guidelines October 1, 2024 (Older): Requirement to link the toxic effect code to the specific condition caused by the poisoning or adverse effect -> icd (New Version): Requirement to code adverse effects and underdosing as distinct entities following the O9A.2 code

findings.section: Anemia Sequencing for Chemotherapy Adverse Effects
findings.summary: The older version (FY25 Guidelines) and the new version (icd) present the exact same coding logic, sequencing requirements, and specific code references (T45.1X5-, Y84.2). There are no functional differences in the clinical rules or code assignment instructions between the two texts provided. The text appears to be a static guideline excerpt repeated in both profiles without any delta in behavior.
findings.detail:
ICD-10-CM FY25 Guidelines October 1, 2024 (Older): Anemia code sequenced first for chemo/immunotherapy adverse effects -> icd (New Version): Anemia code sequenced first for chemo/immunotherapy adverse effects
ICD-10-CM FY25 Guidelines October 1, 2024 (Older): Code Y84.2 required for radiotherapy adverse effects -> icd (New Version): Code Y84.2 required for radiotherapy adverse effects
